Petition For Prior Approval Of Right To Compensation As Personal Representative And For Services As Attorney For The Perosnal Representative
Off Calendar May-11-2009 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jeannette Fisher-Kouadio, Esq. present in pro per as personal representative.

Ms. Fisher-Kouadio stated to the Court that she is ready to withdraw the Petition for dual compensation.
The Court continued the status on filing of correct and final inventory to June 1, 2009 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Comm. Sue Kaplan, CSR: LaVena Ward #7077, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, Bailiff: Deputy Gomez.

Off Calendar Apr-13-2009 Continued To Apr-27-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition By Jeannette Fisher Kouadio Proposed Administrator For Prior Approval Of Right To Compensation As Personal Representative And For Services As Attorney For The Personal Representative
Off Calendar Dec-15-2008 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jeanette Fisher-Kouadio for and with Timolyn Brooks (Petitioner).

Ms. Fisher-Kouadio presented argument to support her position stating that she had been asked to be the attorney and the administrator by the family, that it was burdensome for her client to oversee her mother and her aunt's estate, that her client's brothers could not serve, that the estate will inherit one-third interest of real property from the Sims estate, that the property will need to be sold, and as a representative of both estates she will not be asking for extraordinary fees. The Court stated that the burden was to show that the dual compensation would benefit the estate, that it would be time comsuming to make the estate liquid, and that at this point the estate is paying double. The Court denied the petition without prejudice. Ms. Fisher-Kouadio to prepare the order.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda/R. Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.
